data.title: LivsMed completes largest 2025 KOSDAQ IPO, raising $94M (₩135.9B) at ₩55,000/share for market cap of ₩1.4T ($1B) to expand globally.
data.description: A South Korean minimally invasive surgical device company, LivsMed, has successfully completed its initial public offering on the KOSDAQ market, raising approximately $94 million (₩135.9 billion). Priced at ₩55,000 per share, the IPO has propelled the company's market capitalization to over ₩1.4 trillion (about $1 billion), solidifying its position as a leading Medtech "Unicorn." The funds raised will be utilized for establishing an integrated production facility and supporting continued research and development efforts.

data.title: ImmunityBio sees a 700% revenue surge in 2025 with preliminary net product revenue of $113 million for Anktiva, alongside a 750% increase in unit sales volume compared to 2024.
data.description: ImmunityBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: IBRX) saw a surge in shares on Thursday due to its impressive execution and sales performance, reporting a substantial rise in preliminary net product revenue. For fiscal 2025, the company announced that their product Anktiva brought in around $113 million in net product revenue, marking a remarkable 700% increase from the previous year. Anktiva, the first FDA-approved immunotherapy for non-muscle invasive bladder cancer carcinoma in situ (CIS), exhibited significant growth. In the fourth quarter of 2025, ImmunityBio reported revenue of approximately $38.3 million, surpassing the previous quarter’s $31.8 million, reflecting a 20% quarter-over-quarter increase and a 431% year-over-year increase. Furthermore, the company noted a 750% surge in unit sales volume for Anktiva in 2025 compared to 2024. This exceptional growth showcases the company’s strong quarter-over-quarter revenue progression.

data.title: State Street (NYSE: STT) introduces digital finance infrastructure, gears up for earnings. Analysts predict $2.84 EPS, $3.62B revenue.
data.description: State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) shares saw a slight increase as the company introduced a new digital finance infrastructure to connect traditional and blockchain ecosystems. The stock is currently challenging resistance and hit a new high. The announcement precedes the firm's earnings release on Friday, January 16. Analysts predict the Boston-based company will post quarterly earnings of $2.84 per share, higher than the $2.60 per share reported in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for State Street's quarterly revenue stands at $3.62 billion. For more information, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: BD wins FDA clearance for new breast biopsy system, launching in early 2026. Features advanced capabilities. Market cap $59.10B, stock 52-week range $162.29-$251.99.
data.description: On Thursday, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration cleared Becton, Dickinson and Company's new breast biopsy system, the EnCor EnCompass Breast Biopsy and Tissue Removal System, under the 510(k) process. The system is set to be launched in early 2026, aiming to improve the biopsy process by enabling clinicians to conduct procedures using various breast imaging methods. It offers advanced features such as dual vacuum strengths, 360-degree sampling capability, and multiple probes for different types of lesions. Becton, Dickinson and Company, trading under NYSE: BDX, holds a market capitalization of $59.10 billion, with its stock price fluctuating between $162.29 and $251.99 in the past 52 weeks. This development stands out amidst a generally positive trend in major stock market indices. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson's drug slows blood cancer, extends survival in pretreated patients in a Phase 3 study for multiple myeloma.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) released findings from the Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 study of Tecvayli (teclistamab-cqyv) monotherapy for multiple myeloma, a form of blood cancer. The trial compared Tecvayli against the standard treatments of pomalidomide, bortezomib, and dexamethasone (PVd) or carfilzomib and dexamethasone (Kd) in patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma who had undergone 1 to 3 previous lines of therapy. This study marks the second Phase 3 trial supporting Tecvayli as a potential new standard of care from the first relapse onwards. Significant survival data were observed in the study. For further details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: AddressHate donates $75,000 to Baruch College's Antisemitism Studies Lab, helping catalyze nearly $500,000 in funding.
data.description: Baruch College in New York received a generous donation of $75,000 from AddressHate, a leading non-profit entity specializing in innovative methods to combat online hate. The contribution is designated to bolster The Antisemitism Studies Laboratory situated in Baruch's Sandra Kahn Wasserman Jewish Studies Center. AddressHate's pledge aims to enhance Baruch's capabilities in researching and addressing the growing concern of modern antisemitism, especially in the online sphere.

data.title: CIM Group buys 3 industrial buildings totaling 290,000 sf from Dynatect Manufacturing Inc. in a sale/leaseback deal.
data.description: In Los Angeles, CIM Group has purchased three industrial properties encompassing around 290,000 square feet from Dynatect Manufacturing Inc. in a sale and leaseback deal.

data.title: Hayden AI expands to parking enforcement vehicles in Santa Monica, deployed on 7 vehicles, increasing coverage with over 2,100 buses.
data.description: Hayden AI has achieved a significant milestone by equipping its technology onto seven parking enforcement vehicles in the City of Santa Monica, marking a new vehicle type for the company. This marks the first deployment of Hayden AI's technology on parking enforcement vehicles, expanding from its existing bus-mounted enforcement contract. The installation on these vehicles boosts the reach of this vital safety technology in Santa Monica. Additionally, Hayden AI's technology is currently operational on more than 2,100 public transit buses, highlighting its widespread adoption and impact in the transportation industry.

data.title: Rimmel London and Red Bull collaborate for gravity-defying lashes and thrilling beam routine stunt.
data.description: In London, an exciting partnership between Rimmel London and Red Bull has resulted in a gravity-defying spectacle that showcases thrilling beam routine stunts. The collaboration promises to defy gravity and captivate audiences with daring lash displays. This unique event is set to stun with its extraordinary showcase of courage and innovation.

data.title: CDPH Dashboard confirms silicosis epidemic in California fabrication shops; data shows artificial stone can't be safely fabricated by humans.
data.description: According to a recent report from the California Department of Public Health (CDPH) dashboard, there has been a significant increase in cases of silicosis in fabrication shops throughout the state. The data, released on January 15, 2026, indicates that artificial stone fabrication poses a serious health risk to workers, as it cannot be safely done by human beings. This revelation, provided by Brayton Purcell LLP via a press release on PR Newswire, emphasizes the urgency of addressing this issue. Silicosis cases in California fabrication shops have reached alarming levels, highlighting the need for immediate action to protect workers in this industry.

data.title: Correction: Dayana Velasco appointed as CGO at Aventura Private Wealth for strategic growth, as of Jan 15, 2026.
data.description: Aventura Private Wealth has announced the appointment of Dayana Velasco as the Chief Growth Officer (CGO) to drive strategic expansion. The news was released on 15-Jan-2026 by Aventura Private Wealth via PR Newswire. Following corrections made by the company, the updated release provides comprehensive information on this recent development. For more details, refer to the latest communication from the company.

data.title: DentaQuest donates $30,000 to University of Iowa College of Dentistry for Geriatric and Special Needs Dentistry.
data.description: A contribution of $30,000 has been announced by DentaQuest, a part of Sun Life U.S., on Jan. 15, 2026, in Wellesley, Massachusetts, to support the expansion of the Geriatric and Special Needs Dentistry Program at the University of Iowa College of Dentistry and Dental Clinics.
